arbiter.rationale: The audit supports classification of substring-based domain matching as an OUT_OF_SCOPE security anti-pattern. The mechanism does not establish an exact normalized hostname boundary and must not advance as an adoptable pattern.

governor.rationale: The audit marks the OpenHero source as MIT and compatible, but licensing does not make the rejected mechanism acceptable. This decision authorizes no OpenHero source reuse, no third-party asset reuse, and no implementation. Any future use of unrelated OpenHero source would still require normal Governor review and notice preservation.

steward.rationale: Substring domain matching conflicts with Orchestra security goals and creates no acceptable implementation path. The finding may inform prohibited-behavior documentation or adversarial validation, but the source mechanism must not enter an evolution proposal.

maintainer_decision.rationale: Reject the source mechanism and block implementation. Preserve the audit finding only as a negative security lesson supporting exact URL parsing, approved schemes, normalized hostname comparison, explicit subdomain boundaries, restrictive allowlists, sandboxing, and Content Security Policy controls.
